ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Bandung (rose syrup and evaporated milk drink). FOODON_03301737 (rose syrup drink) is the closest parent category available.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (flavoured milk-based drink) is the closest defensible parent. No specific Bandung code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Bandung or rose syrup milk drink. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One upsize serving of Bandung is approximately 500 ml, equivalent to a large takeaway cup as served at Singaporean hawker stalls and food courts.

nutrient_source.title: Bandung (Rose Syrup Milk Drink) — HPB Singapore Foodo / adapted from evaporated milk and rose syrup composition data
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Foodo entry for Bandung upsize was identified. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo data for sweetened evaporated milk beverages and USDA SR Legacy data for rose syrup and evaporated milk components. The upsize serving (500 g) is a scalar of the standard 250 ml serving. Per-100g values are derived from a typical Bandung formulation: approximately 60% evaporated milk diluted with water and 15% rose syrup by volume. Values are consistent with published Singapore hawker beverage data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — sweetened milk-based beverages and rose syrup analogue
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bandung. GI of 65 is estimated by analogy with sweetened condensed/evaporated milk beverages and sugar-syrup drinks documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Rose syrup is predominantly sucrose (GI ~65). The diluted evaporated milk base moderates the overall GI slightly. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 52.5) / 100 = 34.1 for a 500 g upsize serving containing approximately 52.5 g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~190 mg per upsize serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single upsize Bandung contributes approximately 190 mg sodium, which is within acceptable limits for most hypertensive individuals, but the added sugar load and caloric density should be considered when managing overall dietary quality in hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and medium-to-high glycemic load per upsize serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: An upsize Bandung (500 ml) delivers approximately 50 g of free sugars and a glycemic load of ~34, which can cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption and opt for unsweetened beverages instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from evaporated milk and high free-sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of Bandung contributes saturated fat (~5 g per upsize serving) and a high free-sugar load (~50 g), both of which are associated with dyslipidaemia and increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake to occasional consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bandung is a popular Southeast Asian sweet beverage made by mixing rose syrup (a bright pink, rose-flavoured sugar syrup) with evaporated or fresh milk and water, resulting in a distinctively pink, sweet, and mildly floral drink. It is a staple at Singaporean and Malaysian hawker centres, food courts, and kopitiams, and is commonly served chilled or over ice. The upsize variant (approximately 500 ml) is a larger portion offered at food stalls such as Qi Ji in Singapore. Nutritionally, Bandung is a high-sugar, moderate-calorie beverage: a 500 ml upsize serving provides approximately 310 kcal, 52.5 g carbohydrate (predominantly free sugars from rose syrup), 7.5 g protein, and 7.5 g fat from the evaporated milk base. The glycemic load per upsize serving is approximately 34, classifying it as a high-GL beverage. Clinically, Bandung is of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its high free-sugar content and significant glycemic load. It is also relevant for cardiovascular disease management due to saturated fat from evaporated milk and the high sugar load. Sodium content is moderate. The drink contains milk allergens and is not suitable for lactose-intolerant or dairy-free individuals. It is vegetarian and halal-certified in most Singaporean hawker contexts.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Bandung upsize?
What is rose syrup milk drink?
How much sugar is in Bandung?
Is Bandung suitable for people with high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo or USDA entry for Bandung upsize was identified. Nutrient values are adapted from evaporated milk and rose syrup component data from HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA SR Legacy. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using sweetened milk-based beverage analogues. Confidence is medium because the formulation is well-understood but the exact restaurant-specific recipe (Qi Ji - Funan) is not independently verified.
notes: Upsize serving is approximately 500 ml / 500 g. Per-100g values derived from a standard Bandung formulation (approximately 60% diluted evaporated milk, 15% rose syrup, remainder water). Dietary fibre is 0.0 g as this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per component analysis. Trans fat is 0.0 g; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy evaporated milk data. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB or USDA source data for this beverage type. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this beverage. glycemic_index set to 65 (medium GI) by analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sweetened milk-based beverage data; gi_category medium. GL per upsize serving = (65 × 52.5) / 100 = 34.1. No customization is allowed per the field survey listing (Qi Ji - Funan). Price: SGD 4.00. FoodOn match is closest_parent (rose syrup drink, FOODON_03301737); no exact Bandung term exists. FoodEx2 match is closest_parent (A0CKP, flavoured milk-based drink). SNOMED CT omitted as no suitable code exists.

improvement_actions_taken: Added all mandatory Singaporean local-language names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Derived per-100g nutrient values from HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA SR Legacy component data for evaporated milk and rose syrup.
Set glycemic_index=65 with analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database; documented in gi_source and notes.
Added zero-justification phrases for dietary_fibre_g and trans_fat_g in notes.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes.
Set ontology match types to closest_parent or none only; no invented codes.
Ensured all aliases_by_language entries appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Added comprehensive health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance.
